title: AudioMuse-AI
description: Self-hosted sonic analysis engine that auto-generates intelligent, mood-based playlists for Jellyfin, Navidrome, LMS, Lyrion and Emby - no external services.
installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Flask web/API container ONLY (ghcr.io/neptunehub/audiomuse-ai with SERVICE_TYPE=flask, HTTP port 8000). AudioMuse-AI is a MULTI-CONTAINER stack and is NOT functional with this pod alone - it just serves the UI/API; the actual sonic analysis and playlist generation run in a separate worker. MANDATORY COMPANION SERVICE to deploy as a SEPARATE app using the SAME image (ghcr.io/neptunehub/audiomuse-ai:latest) and the SAME Postgres + Valkey/Redis + analysis volume: (a) the ANALYSIS WORKER - set SERVICE_TYPE=worker and provide identical POSTGRES_* and REDIS_URL env vars; it is an RQ worker that pulls jobs from Redis, performs Librosa/ONNX audio analysis locally, and writes results to Postgres (without it, every analysis/playlist task queues forever). OPTIONAL: a SCHEDULER/beat companion (same image) for periodic re-analysis if you want recurring jobs. MEDIA SERVER: this is an add-on to an EXISTING music server - after first boot, open the web UI at the app URL and configure the connection to your Jellyfin / Navidrome / Emby / LMS server (server URL + API key / credentials) under settings; AudioMuse-AI reads the library from that server, it does not host music itself. ROTATE before going live: the Postgres password (POSTGRES_PASSWORD - change from the template default 'audiomuse-ai'). Valkey/Redis is password-less in-cluster. The /app/temp_audio volume (TEMP_DIR) is scratch space where the worker downloads tracks for analysis and should be persistent/sized for your concurrency. First analysis of a large library is CPU-heavy; give the worker pod a larger size.
